---
title: "Why random-walk beats SPARQL for question answering"
description: "SPARQL fails hard, returning empty, when structure is provisional; random-walk-with-restart degrades gracefully into ranked neighborhoods an LLM can reason over."
date: "2026-07-20"
categories: [retrieval]
draft: true
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status"}
**Design.** The intended approach, not yet implemented.
:::

**Thesis.** SPARQL fails hard, returning empty, when structure is provisional; random-walk-with-restart degrades gracefully into ranked neighborhoods an LLM can reason over.

**Draws on.** Drawn from the wiki's Distribution-vs-Pattern-Retrieval and Content-vs-Topology-Questions.

## Outline

- 
- 
- 

_Stub. Expand from the seed page(s) above._
